2605.10537.title: Mela: Test-Time Memory Consolidation based on Transformation Hypothesis
2605.10537.paper_summary: Memory consolidation, the process by which transient experiences are transformed into stable, structured representations, is a foundational organizing principle in the human brain, yet it remains largely unexplored as a design principle for modern sequence models. In this work, we leverage established neuroscientific theories of memory consolidation and cross-frequency coupling to propose the Hierarchical Memory Module (HMM), a neural memory architecture composed of two functionally distinct sub-modules that operate at different update frequencies. Inspired by the transformation hypothesis, the low-frequency sub-module produces high-level representations that capture abstract, gist-level knowledge, while the high-frequency sub-module produces fine-grained representations that preserve richer episodic detail. The final memory output is dynamically reconstructed as a context-dependent combination of both representations, analogous to the reconstructive nature of human memory retrieval. We integrate HMM into a Transformer-based language decoder to form Mela, a family of memory-augmented language models that perform online memory consolidation at test time. To further exploit the multi-granularity memory representations produced by HMM, we introduce MemStack, a method that distributes different levels of memory features across the early layers of the decoder without introducing additional tokens. Experiments on language modeling demonstrate that Mela outperforms Transformer baselines across all the model sizes. Moreover, with the pretrained context length fixed at 4K, Mela maintains performance on significantly longer contexts, whereas Transformer baselines degrade rapidly beyond their training length. Extensive ablation studies validate the contribution of each component and provide guidance for practical configuration.
2605.10537.paper_summary_zh: 記憶鞏固，將短暫經驗轉化為穩定、結構化表徵的過程，是人類大腦中的一個基礎組織原則，然而作為現代序列模型的設計原則，它仍然在很大程度上未被探索。在這項工作中，我們利用已建立的神經科學記憶鞏固理論和跨頻耦合，提出了分層記憶模組（HMM），這是一種由兩個功能上不同的子模組組成的神經記憶架構，這些子模組在不同的更新頻率下運作。受到轉換假說的啟發，低頻子模組產生捕捉抽象、要點級知識的高層次表徵，而高頻子模組則產生保留更豐富情節細節的細緻表徵。最終的記憶輸出作為兩種表徵的上下文依賴組合動態重建，類似於人類記憶檢索的重建性質。我們將HMM整合到基於Transformer的語言解碼器中，形成Mela，一系列在測試時執行在線記憶鞏固的記憶增強語言模型。為了進一步利用HMM產生的多粒度記憶表徵，我們引入了MemStack，一種在解碼器的早期層中分配不同層次記憶特徵的方法，而不引入額外的標記。語言建模實驗表明，Mela在所有模型大小上均優於Transformer基線。此外，當預訓練的上下文長度固定為4K時，Mela在顯著更長的上下文上保持性能，而Transformer基線在超過其訓練長度後迅速退化。廣泛的消融研究驗證了每個組件的貢獻，並為實際配置提供了指導。
2605.10537.author: Lungchuan Chen et.al.
